definition: A provision of the U.S. Constitution that protects individuals in criminal proceedings in several ways, including the right against self-incrimination (the right to remain silent) and the protection against double jeopardy (being tried twice for the same crime).
examples: Hunter's case rests on the Fifth Amendment's Double Jeopardy Clause, which he argues forbids the government from prosecuting him after he already pleaded guilty under a plea deal.
